summary: Small single-story rectangular building with a stepped dark roof and low surrounding cobblestone border. Walls are mid-height stone bricks with a cobblestone top trim and multiple horizontal windows. Roof is made of dark nether-type blocks with light stone slabs as decorative caps at each roof step.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular box with a centered stepped roof
shape.symmetry: rough bilateral symmetry along long axis (front/back mirror)

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: lower wall (smooth blocks forming main wall courses)

materials.name: cobblestone
materials.role: top wall trim and exterior low border/walk

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: decorative slabs on roof steps (light grey slabs)

materials.name: wood
materials.role: possible door/frame (uncertain: small wooden detail visible near an entrance)

elements: row of horizontal windows on each long side (3-4 panes wide)
single low entrance (uncertain exact position, likely on one short side)
stepped dark roof with slabs capped on each step
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular stonebrick wall core ~11x13 blocks footprint and ~4 blocks tall for interior ceiling.
Place a single-block-high cobblestone course around the top edge of the walls as a trim, then add cobblestone border one block out at ground level.
Install horizontal window groups centered on the long walls (three glass panes per group separated by stonebrick columns).
Build a centered stepped roof using nether brick (or nether brick slabs/stairs) rising three steps to a small flat top; cap each outward roof step with light grey stone slabs.
Leave interior floor and doorway placement flexible; door likely on a short side where a break in windows and a torch/wood detail appears.
uncertainties: Exact footprint dimensions: counted from images but could be off by a block or two.
Material IDs: nether_brick assumed for dark roof and stone slabs assumed for light caps; could be different dark/dense blocks or slabs.
Door location and interior layout are not visible clearly; entrance side inferred from one image showing a wooden detail but not confirmed.
Some decorative details (stairs vs slabs on roof edges) are unclear from angles; stair orientation and slab placement may vary.
